stemHtml: Which of these statements about a trimmable horizontal stabiliser is correct?

options.a: A trimmed aeroplane with an aft CG requires the stabiliser leading edge to be lower than in the case of a forward CG in the same condition.
options.b: Because take-off speeds do not vary with CG position, the need for stabiliser adjustment is dependent on flap position only.
options.c: At the aft CG limit, stabiliser trim is adjusted fully nose up to obtain maximum elevator authority at rotation during take-off.
options.d: A trimmed aeroplane with an aft CG requires the stabiliser leading edge to be higher than in the case of a forward CG in the same condition.
